blockingIssues.description: The atomic ingestion upsert path still unconditionally sets `usageSource = excluded.usageSource` on conflict, so equal-confidence collisions can overwrite canonical source identity. This was a prior blocking issue and remains unresolved.
blockingIssues.evidence: Current code at ParserCheckpointStore.swift:305 still contains `usageSource = excluded.usageSource`, and commit 2590418 modifies only HermesParser.swift (no ParserCheckpointStore changes).

codeReview.summary: This fix resolves the prior Hermes fallback-gating defect by removing `max(summary, estimated)` behavior and applying estimation only when explicit and summary usage buckets are absent. However, the prior blocking source-identity defect in atomic ingestion remains unchanged, so the fix feature does not fully address the original failure set.

issues.description: Atomic ingestion conflict SQL still overwrites `usageSource` on equal-confidence writes.

evidence: Resolved prior issue #2: HermesParser now gates fallback via `hasExplicit`/`summaryHasUsage` in parseGatewayIndex (around lines 388-417) and parseCLISnapshot (around lines 503-521), with no remaining `max(summary..., estimated...)` usage.
Unresolved prior issue #1: ParserCheckpointStore conflict path still sets `usageSource = excluded.usageSource` (line 305), violating source-identity preservation on equal-confidence collisions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Canonical token upsert precedence/source-identity rules are implemented in multiple SQL sites (UsageStore and ParserCheckpointStore) without a single shared contract implementation, making invariant drift likely during fixes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: UsageStore uses guarded CASE logic for usageSource, while ParserCheckpointStore.swift:305 still uses unconditional replacement.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true` despite not following required read steps from the skill procedure, which likely contributed to missing the ParserCheckpointStore path again.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: token-accounting-worker SKILL.md requires reading mission.md, AGENTS.md, architecture.md, and user-testing.md. Transcript skeleton read calls for session 871d4cd8 include mission.md/architecture.md but do not include AGENTS.md or user-testing.md, and do not reference ParserCheckpointStore.swift.

summary: Fail: Hermes fallback gating was fixed, but the original blocking source-identity bug in ParserCheckpointStore remains, so the re-run fix does not fully close the prior failure.
